description: A mutable memory location with dynamically checked borrow rules. Unlike Cell, RefCell works with non-Copy types by lending references. It enforces Rust's borrowing rules at runtime instead of compile time: you can have many shared borrows OR one mutable borrow, but not both. Panics if the rules are violated.

methods.name: new
methods.description: Creates a new RefCell containing the given value.

methods.name: borrow
methods.description: Returns an immutable reference to the inner value. Panics if the value is currently mutably borrowed.

methods.name: borrow_mut
methods.description: Returns a mutable reference to the inner value. Panics if the value is currently borrowed (either mutably or immutably).

methods.name: try_borrow
methods.description: Tries to immutably borrow the value. Returns Err instead of panicking if the value is currently mutably borrowed.

methods.name: try_borrow_mut
methods.description: Tries to mutably borrow the value. Returns Err instead of panicking if the value is already borrowed.

methods.name: into_inner
methods.description: Consumes the RefCell and returns the wrapped value.
methods.example:
let cell = RefCell::new(String::from("hello"));
let s = cell.into_inner();
